errors: [1. The ELM implementation checks for LDL-C > 190 mg/dL for exclusion criteria, but the CPG specifies LDL-C >= 190 mg/dL. 2. The ELM's HasCVDRiskFactors logic for dyslipidemia includes only LDL > 130 mg/dL and HDL < 40 mg/dL, but the CPG defines dyslipidemia as elevated LDL, low HDL, or elevated total cholesterol (missing total cholesterol check).]

[Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age >= 18 years but ELM uses age >= 25 years. Time interval mismatch: CPG requires 12-month lookback for blood pressure screening encounters but ELM uses 6-month lookback.]
[Age range upper limit in ELM is 80 years instead of 85 years as specified in CPG]
The HbA1c threshold in the ELM implementation is incorrectly set to 9 g% instead of the CPG-specified 6.5%. This numeric value does not match the requirement in the Clinical Practice Guideline.

[The ELM implementation does not include the 20 pack-year smoking history requirement or the 15-year quit time frame in the "Has Smoking History" logic. The CPG requires these additional criteria for inclusion, but the ELM only checks for the presence of a tobacco user condition with active status. All other numeric values (age thresholds, 1-year lookback) and value set references match the CPG requirements.]

[Age thresholds in ELM implementation are incorrect (60-75 years) compared to CPG requirements (45-75 years). Time interval for recent colorectal screening in ELM is 2 years instead of the required 1 year from CPG.]

The ELM implementation uses a 6-month lookback period for "Has Recent Anxiety Screening" (checking for screenings in the past 6 months), but the CPG explicitly requires a 1-year lookback period ("screening has been performed within the past 1 year"). This time interval mismatch directly contradicts the CPG requirements.

[Age thresholds in ELM implementation are incorrect. The CPG specifies age 15-65, but ELM uses age >=30. Additionally, the ELM does not include the upper age limit (65) in the demographic check. The Rationale in the ELM is always null, whereas the CPG requires a specific rationale when InPopulation is true.]
[BMI threshold in ELM is 35 kg/m2 instead of 25 kg/m2 as specified in CPG; time interval for recent glucose test in ELM is 1 year instead of 3 years as specified in CPG]

[In Demographic logic incorrectly checks age >= 30 instead of age >= 21 as specified in CPG requirements. The CPG requires screening for women aged 21-65, but ELM implementation only includes 30-65. This creates a mismatch in the lower age boundary for inclusion criteria.]

The ELM implementation uses a 3-month lookback period for recent depression screenings (in Has Recent Adult/Adolescent Depression Screening), but the CPG requires a 1-year lookback period ("within the past 1 year"). This mismatch in time intervals (3 months vs. 12 months) directly contradicts the CPG requirements.

[Age threshold in ELM implementation is 75 years (In Demographic: age >=75), but CPG requires age >=65 years. This creates a discrepancy in inclusion criteria. The time interval (1 year lookback) and value set reference match requirements.]
[Age threshold mismatch: CPG requires age >=18 for inclusion, but ELM implementation uses age >=25. This discrepancy excludes eligible patients aged 18-24 from the target population.]

[The ELM implementation specifies a 6-month lookback period for DEXA scans (Has Recent DEXA Scan) while the CPG requires a 2-year lookback period. This creates a critical mismatch in the exclusion criteria for recent DEXA scans.]
[Age thresholds mismatch: CPG requires age 65-75 for inclusion, but ELM's "In Demographic" incorrectly uses age >=55. The lower age bound in ELM (55) is 10 years below the CPG requirement (65).]

The logical structure in the ELM implementation incorrectly uses an OR operator between Screening Age and Gender in the MeetsInclusionCriteria calculation. The Clinical Practice Guideline explicitly requires ALL inclusion criteria (age >=40 AND female sex AND no mammogram in past 1 year) to be true, but the ELM uses "(Screening Age OR Gender) AND Mammogram" which fails to enforce the required AND relationship between age and gender.

The ELM implementation incorrectly defines "Needs Tobacco Use Screening" as only checking age (In Demographic). According to the CPG, "Needs Tobacco Use Screening" must combine **both** age >=18 **and** absence of recent tobacco screening (1-year lookback). The ELM fails to include the exclusion of recent screening in the logic for "Needs Tobacco Use Screening," leading to incorrect determination of InPopulation. The logical structure is incomplete and does not match the CPG's requirement of `MeetsInclusionCriteria = Age >=18 AND No recent screening`.

[Age upper limit missing in In Demographic (CPG requires age <=80 but ELM only checks age >=50), Smoking history criteria incomplete (CPG requires 20 pack-years and quit within 15 years but ELM only checks active/former smoker status), No validation of pack-year history or 15-year quit window in smoking criteria]

The ELM implementation is missing the requirement to check for a qualifying encounter during the measurement period (from the "Encounter to Screen for Depression" value set 2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916) in the inclusion criteria. The CPG explicitly requires this encounter as part of the inclusion criteria, but the ELM's "Needs Depression Screening" logic does not include this check. All other numeric values (age thresholds, time intervals) and logical operators (AND, NOT) align with the CPG requirements.

[The ELM implementation incorrectly uses "Required Age AND Colonoscopy" for MeetsInclusionCriteria, which would include patients who have had a colonoscopy in the past month. The CPG requires screening only when there is NO colonoscopy in the past month. The correct logic should be "Required Age AND NOT Colonoscopy".]

[The ELM implementation incorrectly uses "Has Recent Falls Assessment" as a condition for needing screening. According to the CPG, screening is needed when there is NO recent falls risk assessment within the past year. The correct logic should be: In Demographic AND NOT Has Recent Falls Assessment. The ELM currently uses "AND Has Recent Falls Assessment" which inverts the intended logic.]

[1. The ELM incorrectly implements the exclusion criteria for Bipolar and Depression Diagnoses. The CPG requires both exclusions to be true (NOT Bipolar AND NOT Depression), but the ELM uses NOT (Bipolar AND Depression), which is logically incorrect. 2. The ELM does not include the required "Qualifying Encounter" check from the CPG's inclusion criteria. The "Encounter to Screen for Depression" value set (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1916) is referenced in the CPG but not used in the ELM logic. 3. The time intervals (1 year) for recent screening lookback periods match the CPG. 4. All value set references in the ELM align with the CPG requirements.]

[The ELM implementation incorrectly uses an AND operator in the MeetsExclusionCriteria logic instead of an OR operator as specified in the CPG. The exclusion criteria require that patients should be excluded if they meet **any** of the two conditions (Connective Tissue Disorder OR Amiodarone use), but the ELM combines them with AND, which would only exclude patients who meet **both** criteria. This misalignment in logical operators directly contradicts the CPG requirements.]

The value set reference in the ELM implementation (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.564) does not match the CPG-specified value set (2.16.840.1.113883.3.464.1003.118.12.1028) for "Fall Risk Assessment". All other numeric values (age threshold of 65, 1-year lookback period) and logical structure (AND operator, exclusion of recent assessments) align with the CPG requirements.

The value set reference for "Alcohol Use Screening" in the ELM implementation (2.16.840.1.113883.3.526.3.1278) does not match the CPG requirement (2.16.840.1.113883.3.600.1542). This mismatch could lead to incorrect identification of alcohol misuse screenings. All other numeric values (age threshold and time interval) and logical operators align with the CPG.
